# #253 - Invalid XML log is generated by BOOST_AUTO_TEST_CASE_TEMPLATE when tests are skipped [Closed]

> Username: innovmetric  
> Created at: 2020-02-19 20:49:22 UTC  
> Updated at: 2020-04-21 14:23:40 UTC  
> Closed at: 2020-04-06 12:21:48 UTC  
> Url: https://github.com/boostorg/test/issues/253  

# Problem description  
* Given that the testsuite contains some template test cases with automated registration  
  * and XML log output is generated  
* When some template test cases are skipped  
* Then the XML log output produces invalid XML.  
  
More precisely, the `name` attribute of the `<TestCase>` tag contains the non sanitized characters `<` and `>`.  
  
# How to reproduce  
Compiler used is:  
* Visual Studio 2017 (v141)  
  
Consider the following test suite  
```c++  
// This test suite produces invalid XML log with boost test from  
// boost 1.72.0 when run with the following command-line parameters:  
//  
// --run_test=!@skip --output_format=XML --log_level=all --log_sink=_log.xml  
  
#define BOOST_TEST_MODULE reproduce_invalid_xml  
  
#include <boost/test/included/unit_test.hpp>  
#include <boost/mpl/list.hpp>  
  
using TestTypes = boost::mpl::list< char, int >;  
  
BOOST_TEST_DECORATOR( *boost::unit_test::label( "skip" ) )  
BOOST_AUTO_TEST_CASE_TEMPLATE( Skipped, T, TestTypes )  
{  
    BOOST_TEST( true );  
}  
  
BOOST_AUTO_TEST_CASE( NonSkipped )  
{  
    BOOST_TEST( true );  
};  
```  
This test suite is run with the following command line arguments:  
```  
--run_test=!@skip --output_format=XML --log_level=all --log_sink=_log.xml  
```  
  
The generated XML log is invalid:  
```xml  
<TestLog><TestSuite name="reproduce_invalid_xml"><TestCase name="Skipped<char>" skipped="yes" reason="disabled"/><TestCase name="Skipped<int>" skipped="yes" reason="disabled"/><TestCase name="NonSkipped" file="c:\r\boost-issue\boost_issue\boost_issue.cpp" line="19"><Info file="c:/r/boost-issue/boost_issue/boost_issue.cpp" line="21"><![CDATA[check true has passed]]></Info><TestingTime>23</TestingTime></TestCase></TestSuite></TestLog>  
```  
  
# Possible fix  
There is a method  `xml_log_formatter::test_unit_skipped()` implemented in the source file [include/boost/test/impl/xml_log_formatter.ipp](https://github.com/boostorg/test/blob/boost-1.72.0/include/boost/test/impl/xml_log_formatter.ipp#L111) as follows:  
  
```c++  
void  
xml_log_formatter::test_unit_skipped( std::ostream& ostr, test_unit const& tu, const_string reason )  
{  
    ostr << "<" << tu_type_name( tu )  
         << " name"    << utils::attr_value() << tu.p_name  
         << " skipped" << utils::attr_value() << "yes"  
         << " reason"  << utils::attr_value() << reason  
         << "/>";  
}  
```  
To ensure that the content of the `p_name` propety is sanitized during the output, we call the `get()` on a property:  
```c++  
void  
xml_log_formatter::test_unit_skipped( std::ostream& ostr, test_unit const& tu, const_string reason )  
{  
    ostr << "<" << tu_type_name( tu )  
         // FIX:  
         << " name"    << utils::attr_value() << tu.p_name.get()  
  
         << " skipped" << utils::attr_value() << "yes"  
         << " reason"  << utils::attr_value() << reason  
         << "/>";  
}  
  
```

---
